reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C-O and C-C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N-H (tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to nitrile oxide), loss of H2O

reagents: phenylacetylene (1.0 equiv, 0.5 M in TBAB/EG 1:5 DES)
feed_streams.reasoning: Dipolarophile dissolved in DES; separate stream to control stoichiometry and prevent premature nitrile oxide dimerization at room temperature

reagents: ethyl nitroacetate (2.0 equiv, 1.0 M in TBAB/EG 1:5 DES)
feed_streams.reasoning: Nitrile oxide precursor in DES; kept separate until mixing at reactor inlet to minimize premature decomposition/dimerization

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide in situ via thermal dehydration; 2 equiv used to drive conversion

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.notes: Forms deep eutectic solvent with ethylene glycol; acts as reaction medium promoting nitrile oxide formation

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.notes: Hydrogen bond donor in DES; may facilitate dehydration of nitroacetate

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal tautomerization/dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide (EtO2C-CNO) as the reactive 1,3-dipole, with loss of water. The DES medium facilitates this process.

Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide with phenylacetylene. The nitrile oxide HOMO reacts with the alkyne LUMO in a regioselective manner to give the 3,5-disubstituted isoxazole (ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate).

stream_logic.reasoning: Dipolarophile in DES; kept separate to control stoichiometry at T-mixer before heated coil
Nitrile oxide precursor in DES; 2× concentration of stream A to achieve 2.0 equiv at equal flow rates, or adjust flow rate ratio accordingly

mixing_order_reasoning: Both reagents are mixed at a T-mixer immediately before entering the heated coil reactor. Rapid mixing ensures uniform stoichiometry. The nitrile oxide is generated in situ only at elevated temperature, so premature dimerization at the mixing point (room temperature) is minimal. Equal flow rates with [B] = 2×[A] or a 2:1 flow rate ratio of B:A achieves the required 2.0 equiv stoichiometry.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species; the reaction proceeds under air in batch.
quench_reasoning: Batch protocol quenches with water for workup purposes only; in flow, the product stream can be collected directly and worked up offline. No reactive intermediates persist after the heated zone.
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism (well-established nitrile oxide [3+2] cycloaddition). The DES viscosity (~50-100 cP at RT, lower at 120°C) may require wider tubing or elevated pressure to maintain reasonable flow rates — pressure drop should be calculated via Hagen-Poiseuille. The exact mechanism of nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate in DES (base-free, catalyst-free) likely involves thermal dehydration facilitated by the hydrogen-bonding network of the DES; this is the rate-limiting step. Two-stream approach is recommended but a single pre-mixed stream could also work since nitrile oxide generation requires elevated temperature. Solubility of phenylacetylene in DES at room temperature should be verified experimentally.

intensification_mandate.flow_justification_basis: Flow provides (1) superior heat transfer in viscous DES medium — rapid heating to 120°C in narrow-bore tubing (1 mm ID, surface-to-volume ~4000 m⁻¹ vs ~50 m⁻¹ in batch vial) eliminates thermal gradients that cause nitrile oxide dimerization; (2) precise residence time control at 15 min prevents over-reaction and decomposition; (3) back-pressure regulator allows operation above solvent boiling point safely; (4) continuous production eliminates batch turnaround, improving space-time yield. Target: ≤7.5 min residence time with ≥83% yield via improved heat transfer and mixing.
